title: PM Lane 193 - PM Intake Field Start Bring-Back Review Queue
authority: repo technical authority; PM lane implementation authority; final integration authority
objective: Expose a compact, phone-first, browser-local field-start bring-back review queue inside the PM intake workbench so Jason can classify returned field-start conversation items as source review, customer/site clarification, lead/resource clarification, or later bounded packet candidate without creating meeting notes, tasks, action items, owners, due dates, commitments, reports, routes, storage keys, exports, hosted calls, or write paths.
scope: Added a `Local Field Start Bring-Back Review Queue` section inside the existing `Local Field Start Customer/Site Questions Quick Review` panel on `/pm-review/import-intake`.
Placed the section immediately after the PM Lane 192 conversation closeout prompt review as a sibling section so Lane 190 through Lane 192 proof remains unchanged.
Derived four linked classification cards from existing field questions and field observation scratchpad state.
Covered source review, customer/site clarification, lead/resource clarification, and later bounded packet candidate buckets.
Kept the section links/cards only: no buttons, no meeting-note capture, no export action, no text inputs, no persisted disclosure state, no new localStorage key, and no backend route.

Extended the focused read-only PM import-intake smoke to prove desktop presence, `390x844` mobile visibility, initial and populated bring-back queue states, four links, zero buttons, no bring-back review queue storage key, no implied-authority link/control text, and zero mutation calls.

Used a read-only sidecar to confirm the safest sibling placement, four-bucket shape, copy-risk boundary, and no-write assertion set while VS Code Codex retained PM implementation and final integration authority.
guardrails: No Supabase, Render, Vercel, or Olares product action.
No browser approval button, approval POST wiring, approval submission, or approval-row creation.
No new export action, filename, payload version, localStorage schema, backend route, handler, service/auth/ingress change, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.

No meeting-note capture, task, action item, owner field, due date field, issue, field authorization, lead or crew assignment, schedule/status write, customer commitment, customer report, durable field record, production tracking, completion evidence, billing, payroll, invoice, accounting, or external finance-system write.
validation: rg -n "PM Lane 193|Local Field Start Bring-Back Review Queue|fieldStartBringBackReviewQueue|pm-field-start-bring-back-review-queue|bring-back review queue|bringBackReviewQueue|mutationRequests\)\.toHaveLength\(0\)" PROJECT_STATUS.md docs/operations ops/agents apps/operations-web
Null-byte check on PM status docs
validation_result: PASS - operations-web typecheck passed; operations-web production build passed with /pm-review/import-intake in the route output; focused Playwright pm import-intake smoke passed cleanly with the Local Field Start Bring-Back Review Queue, 390x844 mobile proof, four queue links, zero buttons, no bring-back review queue storage key, no implied-authority link/control text, and zero mutation calls; packet JSON parsed as 2026-05-17-pm-lane-193; PM Lane 193 guardrail rg passed; null-byte check passed on PM status docs; git diff --check passed with only expected LF-to-CRLF warnings on touched and unrelated files.
sidecar_result: Read-only sidecar Boyle recommended placing the bring-back review queue immediately after the conversation closeout prompt review inside the customer/site questions panel, deriving four buckets from field questions and observations, avoiding meeting-note or accountability language, adding no state/export/route/write path, and proving no storage keys or mutation calls.
